baseline.note: Oracle-achievable ceiling: a perfect reviewer lands every held-out case inside its gold band with a valid {rating, notes} object. Re-baseline after Phase 4.5 if a live framework's best stabilises below 1.0 on the swapped-in interview labels.

cases.desc: The agent's latest judged rating_band_accuracy must be >= baseline (minus tolerance).

cases.assert: A perfect-oracle generate (gold-band midpoint + non-empty note) scores rating_band_accuracy=1.0 over all held-out cases.
An empty/blank emission ({}) scores rating_band_accuracy=0.0 (no saturation trap — empty output is never credited).
A non-{rating, notes} object scores 0.0: an extra key, a non-integer/bool/float rating, an out-of-range rating, or empty notes all fail the strict schema.
The two seed bands are disjoint ([85,100] and [0,30]), so a constant-rating agent scores at most 0.5 — the metric cannot be faked by emitting one fixed number.

The labeled set exercises both ends of the lens: a strong test pinning exact outputs across positive/negative/zero inputs (UT-001, [85,100]) and a vacuous 'is not None' test that cannot catch a wrong result (UT-002, [0,30]).
